page.content: The data from Table 1 presents various genomic metrics across different species, starting with Clostridium sp. (Strain CADE) which has a genome size of 2,713,669, 2,605 ORFs, 27.06% GC content, 333 contigs, 3,119 COGs, 1,993 core genes, 215 unique genes, 90.04 ANIb, and is related to Clostridium sp. Ade.TY. Clostridium algidicarnis (CALG) has a genome size of 2,793,371, 2,588 ORFs, 29.98% GC content, 95 contigs, 2,902 COGs, 2,295 core genes, 216 unique genes, 98.38 ANIb, and is related to Clostridium algidicarnis B3.

page.content: Clostridium perfringens (CPER) shows a genome size of 3,860,191, 3,739 ORFs, 27.72% GC content, 1,018 contigs, 7,720 COGs, 1,737 core genes, 493 unique genes, 98.46 ANIb, and is related to Clostridium perfringens SM101. Pseudomonas fluorescens (PFLU) has a genome size of 5,806,496, 5,839 ORFs, 59.05% GC content, 1,486 contigs, 33,938 COGs, 1,627 core genes, 785 unique genes, 87.4 ANIb, and is related to Pseudomonas fluorescens H14. Pseudomonas syringae (PSYR) features a genome size of 2,744,354, 2,771 ORFs, 57.44% GC content, 642 contigs, and lacks COGs, core genes, unique genes, and ANIb data. Finally, Pseudomonas veronii (PVER) has a genome size of 6,299,241, 5,805 ORFs, 60.49% GC content, 289 contigs, 8,700 COGs, 4,200 core genes, 615 unique genes, 93.89 ANIb, and is related to Pseudomonas veronii R4. In Table 2, the media/sample of Labdanolic acid Mycelial yields a protein yield of 3.76 ± 2.06 μg/g FW, with a total number of spots being 714 ± 19 and 18 differentially accumulated spots. The secretome for Labdanolic acid yields 2.99 ± 0.43 μg/mL, with a total number of spots of 209 ± 18 and 20 differentially accumulated spots. The Control Mycelial shows a protein yield of 2.69 ± 0.42 μg/g FW, with 733 ± 23 total spots but no differentially accumulated spots, while the Control secretome yields 1.44 ± 0.29 μg/mL with 209 ± 6 total spots and no differentially accumulated spots.
